source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI via automated Playwright download. Cover page confirms Merry Maids SPE LLC 2025 FDD.

brand.parent_company: ServiceMaster Systems LLC / ServiceMaster OpCo Holdings LLC / RW Purchaser LLC / Roark Capital

initial_franchise_fee.notes: Flat $55,000 Initial Franchise Fee, payable in lump sum at signing, nonrefundable. Training for two people included in fee. Discounts available: Military (20% off), Affiliate (15% — existing ServiceMaster/Merry Maids/Two Men and a Truck franchisee or employee), Conversion Franchise (15%), Industry Experience (5% — 2+ years owning/employed by similar business). Only one discount may be used. Customer acquisition fee of $250 per existing customer may also apply if territory has active customers. In 2024, actual Initial Franchise Fees collected ranged from $18,750 to $49,500. Technology Fee of $499/month also begins at signing (estimated $998-$1,497 in pre-opening tech fees).

provenance.exact_text: you must pay us an initial franchise fee equal to $55,000 (the 'Initial Franchise Fee') upon execution of the Franchise Agreement.

royalty_rate.notes: Base royalty is 7% of Gross Sales. 'Royalty Incentive' (discretionary, may be changed or discontinued at any time) reduces rate: 6% on Gross Sales between $400K-$500K, 5% on Gross Sales above $500K, all within a single calendar year per individual Franchised Business (not combined across multi-unit owners). National Account revenue excluded from tier calculation but still subject to applicable royalty rate. Resets each calendar year. If franchisee fails to timely renew, royalty increases by 2.5% of Gross Sales (to 9.5%) on a weekly basis until renewal is completed or agreement is terminated.

provenance.exact_text: The Royalty will be 7% of Gross Sales of the Franchised Business until such time as Gross Sales of the Franchised Business exceed $400,000 in a given calendar year.

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Two-component: Ad Fund Contribution 1.3% of Gross Sales (national advertising fund) + Local Marketing Obligation 0.7% of Gross Sales per week on 'Eligible Marketing'. Total mandatory marketing: 2.0% of Gross Sales. Additionally, $6,000 Initial Marketing Obligation in 120-day window around opening.

components.name: Ad Fund Contribution (National Advertising Fund)
components.notes: Contributed to the National Advertising Fund ('Ad Fund').

components.name: Local Marketing Obligation
components.notes: Must be spent on 'Eligible Marketing' as specified by franchisor. If not met, difference must be contributed to Ad Fund.

components.name: Initial Marketing Obligation
components.notes: $6,000 required spend on Eligible Marketing in 120-day window (60 days before to 60 days after opening).

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Clean 2% total ongoing marketing cost (1.3% Ad Fund + 0.7% Local Marketing). Simpler structure than Molly Maid. Failure to meet Local Marketing Obligation triggers mandatory contribution of the shortfall to Ad Fund.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Ad Fund Contribution row and Local Marketing Obligations row

technology_fee.notes: $499 per Franchised Business per month. Covers: MM360 (proprietary CRM), Service Mobility Software (dispatch/scheduling), Microsoft Office 365, learning management system, business intelligence platform, and document repository. Franchisor may change included products and services. Begins at signing of Franchise Agreement (pre-opening). Additional software: QuickBooks Accounting and Payroll annual license ~$475 (separate from Technology Fee).

provenance.item_reference: Item 5 (Technology Fees) and Item 6 (Technology Fee row)
provenance.exact_text: you must begin paying us the monthly Technology Fee, which is $499 per month per Franchised Business.

transfer_fee.formula: 25% of then-current Initial Franchise Fee (currently $13,750) for standard transfer of 50%+ ownership. Reduced to 7.5% if transfer to owner's adult child (18+), $500 if transfer to spouse or to entity formed by existing owners, 15% if transfer due to death of controlling owner (not to spouse/child). Plus franchisor's and affiliates' costs and expenses including attorneys' fees. Non-refundable application fee of 25% of Transfer Fee due with transfer request.
transfer_fee.notes: Transfer Fee is percentage-based, not flat. At current $55,000 franchise fee: standard transfer = $13,750, child transfer = $4,125, death transfer = $8,250, spouse/entity reorganization = $500. Transferee must also attend Initial Training (included in Transfer Fee).

other_fees.notes: Tiered by years of operation: 0-5 years: 10% of then-current Initial Franchise Fee ($5,500); 5.01-10 years: 8% ($4,400); 10.01-15 years: 5% ($2,750); 15+ years: no renewal fee. New franchisees get 5-year initial term with option for two 5-year renewals.
For additional trainees, subsequent trainees, or transferees beyond the two included in Initial Franchise Fee or Transfer Fee.
Up to $500/trainee/program for virtual; $250/trainee/day at franchisor's office; $750/trainer/day + travel/living expenses for on-site training.

Up to $1,500 per person for annual conventions, regional meetings, or road shows. Currently no fee charged for meetings other than annual convention. 150% penalty fee if you fail to attend two consecutive annual conventions without valid business reason.
Payable if participating in National Accounts Program.
Plus actual expenses. Additional $500 + travel costs if on-site review required.
Payable if franchisor refers a qualified lead who purchases franchise within 18 months. Not a Transfer Fee.
For non-control transfers, entity name changes, DBA changes, entity form changes, agreement modifications. No charge for DBA or entity form change in first year. Fee may not exceed $750.
$5,000 plus cost of audit, underpayment amount, and interest. Only payable if audit shows understatement of at least 5% of Gross Sales for any given month.
Interest: 2% per month compounded daily (or max permitted by law). Late fees: $200/week for first four late weeks, $500/week thereafter, plus all costs including bank charges and attorneys' fees.
Up to 150% of costs and expenses franchisor incurs to obtain and maintain insurance on your behalf if you fail to maintain required coverage.
$500 plus costs/expenses. May increase to $750. Charged if franchisor intervenes in customer complaint.
Up to 120% of costs/expenses franchisor incurs to perform your obligations after default.
Average monthly Royalties and Ad Fund Contributions over past 12 months times lesser of remainder of term or 24 months. Payable if franchisor terminates due to your default.

line_items.notes: May vary with discounts (see Item 5). Does not include customer acquisition fee of $250/customer. Includes Initial Training for two people.

line_items.category: Travel and Living Expenses During Training
line_items.notes: 10-day initial training in Memphis, TN or designated location. Low for 1 person, high for 2 people. Training may be conducted remotely.
First 3 months rent, security deposit, and leasehold improvements. Typical office: 450-1,800 sq ft. Estimated rent: $6,000-$12,000/year.
QuickBooks license ($475), iPad tablet ($300-$900), optional laptop ($1,000-$1,400), 2-3 months Technology Fees ($900-$1,485).
Office furniture, fixtures, washer and dryer, office decorations.
Cleaning products, equipment, supplies, business cards, brochures, printed materials, uniforms.
First year insurance prepayment including general liability, auto, fidelity bond, workers' comp, employment practices liability.
Background checks and drug screening for initial employees.
Initial Marketing Obligation: $6,000 required spend in 120-day window around opening.
Business license, security deposits, utilities, insurance deposit, incorporation fee, internet (~$100/month), stationery/forms/collateral (~$1,000 for 3 months).
Legal and accounting advice for entity formation, lease negotiation, FDD review, business plan, hiring practices.

Employee salaries/wages/benefits/uniforms, payroll taxes, licenses, phone/internet/utilities, vehicle payments/taxes/licensing, bank charges, taxes, advertising, supplies/equipment, Technology Fees, credit card processing.

item_7_initial_investment.notes: Line item highs sum to $171,010, which is $900 higher than the stated total of $170,110. The stated total from the FDD is used as authoritative. Discrepancy likely in rounding of one or more line items in the published table.

tables.description: Gross Sales for Qualified Franchises in the Covered Period
tables.population: 306 Qualified Franchises (territories with 40,000+ Qualified Households and office within territory) out of 725 Active Franchises out of 802 total Franchised Businesses. Excludes: 42 not operating full 12 months, 35 transferred during period, 419 Legacy Franchises (Active but not 'Qualified'), and 90 that ceased operating.
tables.notes: Only 306 of 802 total Franchised Businesses are represented (38.2%). 419 'Legacy Franchises' (likely smaller/older territories not meeting current 40K+ QH standard) are excluded entirely. Average Gross Sales of $487,441. Median of $427,425. Note: 90 Franchised Businesses that ceased operating during 2024 are excluded — none opened within 12 months prior to closing.

tables.description: Gross Sales for Franchise Ownership Groups with Active Franchises in the Covered Period
tables.population: 249 Franchise Ownership Groups (FOGs) with at least one Active Franchise, out of 323 total FOGs. Excludes 74 FOGs not reporting full 12 months and 8 FOGs that ceased all operations.
tables.notes: FOG-level data shows multi-unit owners generate significantly more total revenue. Average FOG Gross Sales: $1,047,237, but median is $767,299, indicating right-skew from large multi-unit operators. Top FOG generated $5.87M. Includes both Qualified and Legacy Franchises.

tables.description: Gross Sales by Number of Active Franchises in Franchise Ownership Group in the Covered Period
tables.population: 249 FOGs / 725 Active Franchises. Breakdowns by 1, 2, 3-4, 5-7, and 8-24 Active Franchises per FOG.
tables.notes: Key insight: per-franchise average Gross Sales DECREASES as FOG size increases ($547K for single-unit vs $288K for 8-24 unit FOGs), suggesting multi-unit operators hold many smaller/legacy territories. Single-unit operators have highest per-franchise median ($515K). System-wide per-franchise average across all 725 Active Franchises: $347,990, median $262,519.

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 is disclosed with three tables. Table 1 (Qualified Franchises only) is the most relevant for new franchisees since it reflects current territory standards (40K+ QH). However, only 306 of 802 franchises (38.2%) qualify as 'Qualified Franchises.' 419 Legacy Franchises are excluded from Table 1 but included in Tables 2 and 3. Table 3 reveals per-franchise revenue for ALL Active Franchises: average $347,990, median $262,519. The gap between Qualified Franchise average ($487K) and all-franchise average ($348K) suggests Legacy Franchises significantly underperform. 90 businesses that ceased operations in 2024 are excluded from all tables — severe survivorship bias given 802 total units. No cost data, margins, or profitability disclosed.

years_reported.notes: All franchised, no company-owned. Net contraction of 43 units. 47 terminations is a very high number — 4.8% termination rate. Largest state losses: California 137→125 (13 terminations), Illinois 49→46 (3), New Jersey 31→28 (3), Arizona 28→25 (3), Washington 27→24 (3).

Net contraction of 62 units — accelerating decline. Terminations dropped to 16 but non-renewals (24) and ceased operations (35) surged. 66 transfers indicates significant ownership turnover. Largest losses: California 125→107 (9 non-renewals, 9 ceased), Massachusetts 35→27 (9 ceased), Michigan 15→5 (5 terminations, 3 non-renewals, 3 ceased).

Net contraction of 82 units — worst year of the three. Ceased operations exploded to 73 (8.3% of start). Terminations and non-renewals dropped but the 'ceased operations - other reasons' category absorbed the exits. Major state losses: California 107→89 (6 terminated, 5 non-renewals, 7 ceased), Pennsylvania 37→28 (1 non-renewal, 8 ceased), New Jersey 28→21 (7 ceased), New York 35→31 (6 ceased), Ohio 25→19 (6 ceased), Connecticut 20→14 (6 ceased), Florida 64→58 (1 terminated, 6 ceased), Illinois 45→38 (1 terminated, 6 ceased), Virginia 31→26 (5 ceased). Projected 7 new franchised outlets in 2025 (only 1 agreement signed but not open).

action_types.notes: The People of the State of California v. Arby's Restaurant Group, Inc. (2019). Affiliated program, not Merry Maids. Settlement regarding no-poach franchise agreement provisions. ARG paid no money but agreed to remove disputed provision.
The People of the State of California v. Dunkin' Brands, Inc. (2019). Affiliated program, not Merry Maids. Similar no-poach settlement. DBI denied liability.

New York v. Dunkin' Brands, Inc. (2019). Affiliated program. NYAG filed re: credential-stuffing cyberattacks on DD Perks accounts (2015, 2018). Consent agreement: $650,000 in penalties/costs, security program requirements through September 2026.

item_3_litigation.notes: All three disclosed actions involve affiliated programs (Arby's and Dunkin'), NOT Merry Maids itself. FDD states: 'None of these actions have any impact on us or our brand nor allege any unlawful conduct by us.' No direct Merry Maids litigation disclosed. Statement: 'Other than these actions, no litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.' Clean litigation profile for the brand itself.

provenance.exact_text: Other than these actions, no litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.
Our predecessor, Merry Maids Limited Partnership ('Predecessor'), a Delaware limited partnership, offered Merry Maids® franchises between 1980 and December 2020.

warnings: SEVERE SYSTEM CONTRACTION: Merry Maids has shrunk every year for 3 consecutive years with ACCELERATING decline (989→946→884→802). Net loss of 187 units over 3 years (-18.9%). 2024 was the worst year with 82 net losses.

MASSIVE CEASED OPERATIONS: 73 units 'ceased operations - other reasons' in 2024 alone (8.3% of start-of-year count). This is by far the largest exit category and is dramatically higher than 2022 (0) and 2023 (35).

HIGH TURNOVER WARNING: Wisconsin state-mandated risk disclosure (page 4) states: 'During the last three years, 213 outlets were terminated, not renewed, reacquired, or ceased operations for other reasons. This franchise could be a higher risk investment than a franchise in a system with a lower turnover rate.'

SURVIVORSHIP BIAS: Item 19 excludes 90 Franchised Businesses that ceased operating in 2024 from all financial performance tables. Additionally, 419 Legacy Franchises are excluded from Table 1 (Qualified Franchises only), showing only 306 of 802 units.

CONFIDENTIALITY CLAUSES: FDD discloses that current and former franchisees have signed confidentiality provisions 'restricting their ability to speak openly about their experiences.' This may limit due diligence.

NOT NEIGHBORLY/KKR: Merry Maids is owned by RW Purchaser LLC / Roark Capital Management, operating through ServiceMaster Systems LLC securitization structure. It is NOT part of the Neighborly/KKR family (unlike Molly Maid).
Royalty Incentive tiers (6% above $400K, 5% above $500K) are discretionary and 'may be changed or discontinued at any time.' Base rate is 7%.
Item 7 line items high column sums to $171,010, which is $900 more than the stated total of $170,110. Stated total used as authoritative.
MANDATORY MINIMUM PAYMENTS: Wisconsin risk disclosure notes 'You must make minimum royalty or advertising fund payments, regardless of your sales levels.'
SALES PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENT: Wisconsin risk disclosure notes minimum sales performance levels must be maintained or territorial rights may be lost / franchise terminated.
INDEPENDENT FRANCHISEE ORGANIZATION: MMFOA (Merry Maids Franchise Owners Association) exists through AAFD. This is an independent organization, not franchisor-sponsored.
Transfer Fee is percentage-based (25% of then-current Initial Franchise Fee = $13,750 at current $55K fee), which scales with future fee increases.

null_fields.field: raw.item_6_other_fees.royalty_rate.value set to 7.0 but actual effective rate varies
null_fields.reason: Base rate is 7% but discretionary Royalty Incentive reduces to 6% ($400K-$500K) and 5% (above $500K). Marked ambiguous.
Percentage-based formula (25% of then-current Initial Franchise Fee), not a fixed dollar amount.
